MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathleen S. Morris of the Office of the City Attorney, 554-3987, appearing for defendant and moving party
Frederick C. Roesti, 552-3733, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and SANDIVAR HETZER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant has established that plaintiff can present no evidence that defendant's negligence caused the accident or that defendant had any actual or constructive notice of this floor mat being in an unsafe condition. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Sara Lerschen, CSR 6213

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2006 Continued To Aug-07-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Frederick Roesti, 552-3733, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party
John Aubrey of the Office of the City Attorney, 554-3968, appearing for defendants and moving parties

The Court made a personal disclosure and the matter was argued. The Court granted defendant Sandivar Hetzer's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. The Court continued the Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication as to defendant City and County of San Francisco to August 7, 2006. Supplemental opposition and a supplemental reply may be filed and hand served by August 2, 2006 and August 3, 2006 respectively. Plaintiff's request for sanctions and attorneys fees is denied. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Re-Open Discovery For Liminted Purpose
Off Calendar Jun-29-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Fredrick Christian Roesti, for Plaintiff/Moving party
Kathleen S. Morris, for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Re-Open Discovery For Limited Purpose is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is moot as Defendant has provided Plaintiff with copies of the photos that Plaintiff attached to his claim. The court allows discovery to be reopened as to the custodian who was assigned to the station area as it is relevant to the issues raised by the amended complaint. As to the remainder of the requests made in the motion, the court finds that Plaintiff has not been diligent in pursuing discovery. The court also finds that there has been no good cause established for the failure to seek the requested further discovery prior to the arbitration. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
